---
title: "NVIDIA Drivers: No Devices Detected"
date: 2011-06-06
forum: Multimedia Software
---

### Post by divey on 2011-06-06
I'm having issues getting the nvidia-current drivers working with a GeForce 8400 graphics card on Ubuntu 10.10 (x64). 
After running nvidia-xconfig and restarting, X fails to start with an error: No devices detected.

Here is my xorg.conf:
```

# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 260.19.06  (buildmeister@builder101)  Mon Sep 13 04:59:45 PDT 2010

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0"
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "Unknown"
    HorizSync       28.0 - 33.0
    VertRefresh     43.0 - 72.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BusID	   "PCI:d:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection

```

I tried specifying the BusID (from lspci) in the xorg.conf file but it didn't help :(.

I have two VGA devices in lspci, my 8400 GS and the Sun X4170 M2 server integrated graphics:


Thanks,
Derek

---

### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2011-06-07
I think the busid needs to be in decimal for xorg.conf even though lspci reports hex..

So try lucky number 13 (dec).

You could also backup/rename/delete your xorg.conf & re-run (sudo) nvidia-xconfig tool.

Your situation is a little different to most because you have (2) video cards ..
Are you trying to use both ?
If not you could try to disable in BIOS.

---

### Post by divey on 2011-06-07
No, I only want to use the NVIDIA card because the server integrated graphics card sucks. I'll try specifying it in decimal form and see what happens. Also, I've deleted xorg.conf multiple times and re-ran nvidia-xconfig and that didn't help unfortunately. I had no xorg.conf to begin with.

---

### Post by divey on 2011-06-07
> **BicyclerBoy said:**
> I think the busid needs to be in decimal for xorg.conf even though lspci reports hex..

So try lucky number 13 (dec).

You could also backup/rename/delete your xorg.conf & re-run (sudo) nvidia-xconfig tool.

Your situation is a little different to most because you have (2) video cards ..
Are you trying to use both ?
If not you could try to disable in BIOS.

Hey,

Thanks a lot for your help. You were correct, I had to specify the Bus ID as 13:0:0 and it worked. 

Thanks again.

Derek

---

### Post by StealthRedneck on 2012-01-19
I know this is an old topic, but it seems I've come into this same issue with hybrid graphics cards. It would appear that after installing [devdriver_4.0_linux_64_270.40.run]("http://developer.download.nvidia.com/compute/cuda/4_0_rc2/drivers/devdriver_4.0_linux_64_270.40.run"), it asks to automatically update the xorg.conf file. MISTAKE! That's when startx no longer works...

:( Of course I played around too much and even my xorg.conf.backup is no good. 

lspci lists the following:

00:02.0 VGA compatible controller: Intel Corporation 2nd Generation Core Processor Family Integrated Graphics Controller (rev 09)

01:00.0 VGA compatible controller: nVidia Corporation GF106 [Geforce GT 555M SDDR3] (rev a1)

Doesn't the nVidia hex code simply convert to "1"? I tried that with the BusID command and it didn't seem to help any. Nor did changing the driver name to "nv" which I saw in another forum. The Xorg log file just shows the (EE) No devices detected.

If anyone can help, please do! :popcorn: I just want CUDA fun :(

---

### Post by mips on 2012-01-19
Your issue is different I think.

Uninstall your nvidia drivers and then look at this [http://www.ivegotavirus.com/blog/2011/11/06/how-to-get-optimus-working-on-ubuntu-11-10-oneiric/](http://www.ivegotavirus.com/blog/2011/11/06/how-to-get-optimus-working-on-ubuntu-11-10-oneiric/)
